sections.paragraph:
- Original: ὁράω

- Transliteration: Horao

- Phonetic: hor-ah'-o

- Definition:

1. to see with the eyes

2. to see with the mind, to perceive, know

3. to see, i.e. become acquainted with by experience, to experience

4. to see, to look to

a. to take heed, beware

b. to care for, pay heed to

5. I was seen, showed myself, appeared

- Origin: properly, to stare at [cf G3700]

- TDNT entry: 10:15,7

- Part(s) of speech: Verb

- Strong's: Properly to stare at (compare G3700) that is (by implication) to discern clearly (physically or mentally); by extension to attend to; by hebraism to experience; passively to appear: - behold perceive see take heed.
